title: 10 best herbal teas for lost voice
intro_desc: Herbal teas for lost voice are infusions made from plants and fruits, which can help soothe and relieve irritation in the throat, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. These teas often contain ingredients such as slippery elm, licorice root, marshmallow root, and wild cherry bark, all of which have soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. By drinking these teas, individuals can alleviate the symptoms of a lost voice, such as hoarseness, dryness, and discomfort. As a result, they can improve their ability to speak and communicate, enhancing their daily lives, particularly in professional and social situations. For example, a singer may rely on these teas to recover from a cold and prevent strain on their vocal cords, while a teacher may use them to soothe a sore throat and maintain their voice throughout the school day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with lost voice because it contains antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Thyme is also known to stimulate the production of mucus, which can help coat and protect irritated throat tissues, while its natural expectorant properties can help break up and expel excess mucus from the lungs. Furthermore, thyme tea is a natural source of flavonoids, which have anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it an ideal remedy for a lost voice. Drinking thyme tea can provide relief from symptoms of a lost voice, such as hoarseness, dryness, and discomfort, and can help promote faster healing and recovery.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Thyme tea helps relax throat muscles, reducing spasms and coughing fits that can further irritate a lost voice. This property is due to the presence of compounds such as thymol and carvacrol.

Expectorant: Thyme tea promotes the removal of mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and making it easier to speak. The expectorant effect is attributed to the essential oils found in thyme.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea reduces inflammation in the throat, providing relief from pain and irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in thyme.

Antimicrobial: Thyme tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause throat infections, helping to prevent and treat a lost voice. The antimicrobial properties are primarily due to the presence of thymol and other essential oils.

Antioxidant: Thyme tea helps protect throat tissues from oxidative damage, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further damage. The antioxidant effects are attributed to the high levels of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and other polyphenols found in thyme.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried thyme leaves to a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the hot water over the thyme leaves and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the infuser or strainer, discarding the used thyme leaves.
Sip the warm thyme tea as needed throughout the day for a soothed and relieved throat.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in aromatic oils, including thymol, which helps soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids and volatile oils that have antispasmodic, antitussive, and expectorant properties, making them helpful for a lost voice.
Stems: Thyme stems contain essential oils and tannins that have astringent properties, which can help relieve irritation and inflammation in the throat.
Seeds: Thyme seeds are a rich source of essential oils, such as thymol and carvacrol, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, beneficial for a lost voice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with a lost voice because it contains mucolytic and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated throat tissues and reduce inflammation. The tea's demulcent qualities help to coat and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, providing immediate relief from irritation and discomfort. Furthermore, plantain tea is rich in vitamins A and C, which are essential for maintaining a healthy immune system and promoting tissue repair. Drinking plantain tea can help to alleviate symptoms of a lost voice, such as hoarseness, soreness, and dryness, and promote faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea helps reduce inflammation in the throat, providing relief for a lost voice by soothing irritation and reducing swelling, thanks to its active constituents like mucilage, flavonoids, and tannins.
Demulcent: Plantain tea creates a protective film over the mucous membranes in the throat, easing pain and discomfort caused by a lost voice, due to the presence of mucilage.

Antimicrobial: Plantain tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses, helping to prevent and treat infections that can cause a lost voice, with the help of active compounds like allantoin, aucubin, and asperuloside.
Expectorant: Plantain tea promotes the expulsion of mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and making it easier to speak, as a result of its high mucilage content.

Antioxidant: Plantain tea neutralizes free radicals and supports the immune system, which aids in the recovery of a lost voice and overall throat health, owing to its rich supply of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other antioxidant compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two large plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a clean, pesticide-free source.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly, then tear them into small pieces and place in a pot.
Add 4 cups of water to the pot, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the plant material, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Consume 3-4 cups of the plantain tea daily, until voice returns to normal.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins and minerals, plantain leaves can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: High in mucilage, plantain seeds can help coat and soothe irritated mucous membranes, making them ideal for lost voice.
Flowers: Plantain flowers contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful for treating respiratory infections and lost voice.
Bark: Plantain bark has been used traditionally to treat throat infections and coughs, making it a good choice for lost voice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great mullein tea helps with a lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritation and reduce swelling in the throat. This herbal tea contains mucilage, a gel-like substance that coats and protects the mucous membranes, providing relief to a sore or inflamed throat. Furthermore, mullein tea is known to have expectorant qualities, which can help loosen and expel mucus, making it easier to breathe and speak. The soothing and calming effects of great mullein tea on the throat and respiratory system can be beneficial in restoring a lost voice.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Great mullein tea helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel mucus and reducing strain on the vocal cords. This property is due to the saponins and mucilage present in the leaves.

Anti-inflammatory: The tea can reduce inflammation in the throat, alleviating pain and irritation, thus promoting a quicker recovery of the voice. This is attributed to the verbascoside and flavonoids found in the plant.
Demulcent: Great mullein tea forms a soothing film over mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and reducing coughing. The demulcent effect is primarily due to the mucilage content.

Antispasmodic: The tea can help relax the muscles of the respiratory tract, reducing spasms and coughing fits, further alleviating strain on the vocal cords. This is due to the presence of verbascoside and rotenone.

Antimicrobial: Great mullein tea can help protect against throat infections, preventing further damage to the vocal cords. This property is attributed to the rotenone, verbascoside, and flavonoids found in the plant.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried mullein leaves and add them to a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the mullein leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Drink the mullein tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to soothe a sore or lost voice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the great mullein plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for a lost voice due to their demulcent and expectorant properties, which help soothe irritated mucous membranes and promote the expulsion of mucus.
Flowers: The bright yellow flowers of the great mullein plant contain mucilage, making them useful for making teas that soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Seeds: The seeds of the great mullein plant are often used in teas to treat a lost voice because they contain saponins, which have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to clear phlegm and reduce swelling in the throat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cowslip tea helps with a lost voice because it contains mucilage, a substance that coats and soothes irritated mucous membranes in the throat. This herbal tea has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help reduce inflammation and remove excess mucus from the respiratory tract. Additionally, cowslip tea contains flavonoids and saponins, which are known to have a calming effect on the throat and vocal cords, making it an ideal remedy for people who have lost their voice due to overuse, strain, or inflammation. Drinking cowslip tea can help relieve symptoms of a lost voice, such as hoarseness, dryness, and discomfort, allowing the voice to recover more quickly.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cowslip tea helps reduce inflammation in the throat, providing relief for a lost voice. This property is due to the presence of saponins and flavonoids.
Expectorant: Cowslip tea aids in the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, helping to clear a hoarse or lost voice. This is attributed to its saponin content.
Demulcent: Cowslip tea forms a soothing film over mucous membranes, reducing irritation and promoting voice recovery. This effect is largely due to its mucilage content.
Analgesic: Cowslip tea helps alleviate pain, providing comfort to a sore throat and reducing discomfort while speaking. This property is attributed to its tannin content.
Antispasmodic: Cowslip tea helps relax muscles, reducing spasms and cramping in the throat, which can contribute to a lost voice. This is due to the presence of alkaloids and saponins.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried cowslip flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the cowslip flowers.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, until voice returns to normal.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and soothing properties, cowslip flowers help reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, making them ideal for treating a lost voice.
Leaves: High in mucilage, cowslip leaves create a protective barrier in the throat, helping to alleviate irritation and promote voice recovery.
Stems: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, cowslip stems aid in reducing swelling and discomfort in the throat, contributing to a faster recovery of lost voice.
Roots: Packed with saponins, cowslip roots produce a gentle expectorant effect, helping to clear phlegm and soothe a sore throat, which is beneficial for restoring a lost voice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound tea helps with lost voice because it contains compounds that are believed to have expectorant and cough-suppressant properties. These compounds can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to speak. Additionally, horehound tea can help to stimulate the production of saliva, which can help to keep the throat moist and lubricated. This can be especially helpful for people who have lost their voice due to overuse or dryness. Furthermore, horehound tea has been used traditionally to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma, which can also contribute to a lost voice.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Horehound tea helps to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel mucus and reduce inflammation, thanks to its active constituents, such as diterpene glycosides and flavonoids.

Antitussive: Horehound tea has a soothing effect on the throat and helps to suppress coughing, which is particularly useful for a lost voice, due to its active constituents, such as marrubiin and volatile oils.

Anti-inflammatory: Horehound tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, allowing for a quicker recovery of a lost voice, due to its active constituents, such as phenolic acids and tannins.
Antimicrobial: Horehound tea has antimicrobial properties that help to fight off infections that can cause a lost voice, thanks to its active constituents, such as alkaloids, saponins, and tannins.

Demulcent: Horehound tea has a soothing effect on irritated and inflamed mucous membranes, providing relief for a sore and scratchy throat, contributing to the recovery of a lost voice, due to its active constituents, such as mucilage and saponins.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 quart of water and 2 tablespoons of dried horehound leaves, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a teapot or mug, discarding the horehound leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and allow the tea to cool slightly.
Sip the tea slowly, aiming to drink 1 cup every 2 hours, for a total of 3-4 cups throughout the day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Horehound leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for lost voice due to their expectorant and demulcent properties, which help soothe irritated throats and expel mucus.

Flowers: Horehound flowers are used in teas for lost voice because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat.

Seeds: The seeds of the horehound plant are rich in essential oils and have been used in teas for lost voice to help stimulate circulation, boost immunity, and provide relief from respiratory symptoms.
Stems: Horehound stems contain marrubiin, a compound known for its expectorant and stimulant properties, making them useful in teas for lost voice to help clear airways and relieve congestion.
Bark: The bark of the horehound plant is used in medicinal teas for lost voice due to its astringent and antispasmodic properties, which help soothe coughs and reduce spasms in the respiratory tract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with a lost voice because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. This compound can help reduce inflammation in the throat, providing relief to irritated vocal cords and reducing discomfort. Additionally, licorice tea can help stimulate the production of mucus, which can help keep the throat lubricated and hydrated, further aiding in the recovery of a lost voice. The tea's warm temperature can also help soothe a sore throat and alleviate any pain or discomfort associated with lost voice. Drinking licorice tea can be a natural and effective way to help restore a lost voice and promote overall throat health.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Licorice tea forms a protective film on the mucous membranes of the throat, providing a soothing effect and reducing irritation, which is important for lost voice as it helps to reduce coughing and soreness. This property is attributed to the presence of glycyrrhizin and other polysaccharides.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea helps reduce inflammation in the throat, alleviating pain and swelling, which is crucial for lost voice as it promotes healing and recovery. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, isoflavonoids, and chalcones.

Expectorant: Licorice tea helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and speak, which is important for lost voice as it aids in clearing congestion and phlegm. The expectorant properties are primarily due to glycyrrhizin and its derivatives.

Antispasmodic: Licorice tea helps relax the smooth muscles of the respiratory tract, reducing spasms and coughing, which is beneficial for lost voice as it minimizes strain and fatigue on the vocal cords. The antispasmodic effects are attributed to the presence of glycyrrhetic acid and its derivatives.

Immunomodulatory: Licorice tea supports the immune system, helping the body fight off infections that can cause a lost voice. This property is important for lost voice as it assists in treating the underlying cause of voice loss and preventing further complications. The immunomodulatory effects are mainly due to the triterpenoid saponins, such as glycyrrhizin and its metabolites.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 1-2 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which helps soothe sore throats and relieve inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes have expectorant properties, making them useful for treating a lost voice due to excessive mucus.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant have demulcent properties, which help soothe and protect irritated mucous membranes in the throat.
Seeds: Licorice seeds contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce throat inflammation and promote healing.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains glycyrrhizic acid, which has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, useful for treating a lost voice caused by infection.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry tea helps with a lost voice because it contains natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Elderberries are also rich in vitamins A, C, and E, which can help boost the immune system and fight off infections that may be causing the loss of voice. Additionally, elderberry tea can help stimulate the production of mucus, which can help keep the throat moist and relieve dryness that can contribute to a lost voice. Drinking elderberry tea regularly may help reduce the duration and severity of a lost voice, making it a popular natural remedy for those experiencing voice loss.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry tea contains flavonoids and anthocyanins that reduce inflammation, helping soothe a sore throat and alleviate hoarseness.
Immune-boosting: Rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as antioxidants, elderberry tea strengthens the immune system, helping the body fight off viruses that cause a lost voice.
Antiviral: Elderberry tea has been shown to inhibit viral replication, particularly for influenza viruses, which can contribute to a lost voice.
Expectorant: Elderberry tea stimulates mucus secretion and has a mild diuretic effect, helping to clear airways, reduce congestion, and soothe irritation.
Demulcent: Elderberry tea contains tannins and mucilage, providing a soothing, protective coating to the throat, reducing irritation and pain.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of dried elderberries, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove from heat and let the mixture steep for an additional 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea, then stir in honey or lemon juice to taste.
Drink the tea while warm, up to 3 times a day, for a maximum of 5 days.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Elderberry flowers are rich in flavonoids and tannins, making them effective in soothing a sore throat and reducing inflammation.
Berries: Elderberries are packed with antioxidants and vitamins that help boost the immune system, providing relief for a lost voice caused by cold or flu.
Leaves: Elderberry leaves contain high levels of tannins and essential oils, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, useful for treating throat irritations.
Stems: Although less potent than the flowers or berries, elderberry stems still contain beneficial compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of a lost voice.
Bark: Elderberry bark is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, making it suitable for treating respiratory issues and soothing a sore throat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with a lost voice because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery when it comes into contact with water. This mucilage coats the throat and soothes irritation, reducing inflammation and providing relief to sore throats. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help to speed up the healing process and restore the voice. The tea is also caffeine-free and hydrating, making it a gentle and soothing choice for those experiencing voice loss. Drinking marshmallow tea can provide immediate relief and long-term benefits for those suffering from a lost voice.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, a substance that becomes a slippery gel when mixed with water, which coats and soothes irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and pain.

Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in marshmallow tea exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate swelling and redness in the throat, facilitating voice recovery.
Expectorant: Marshmallow tea aids in expelling mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and speak by reducing congestion and phlegm accumulation in the throat.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of marshmallow tea, attributed to its polysaccharides and flavonoids, help protect the throat from infections that can exacerbate a lost voice or make it difficult to speak.
Hydration: Drinking marshmallow tea helps maintain adequate hydration levels, which is crucial for a healthy throat and efficient mucus clearance, supporting voice recovery.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of dried marshmallow root with 4 cups of water, then bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 2 tablespoons of dried thyme, and steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea, then add honey to taste.
Drink 3-4 times a day, as needed, while symptoms persist.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Marshmallow leaves are often used in medicinal teas for lost voice due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which helps to coat and soothe irritated throats.
Roots: Marshmallow roots are rich in mucilage, making them an excellent choice for treating a lost voice and soothing irritated mucous membranes.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds are a popular ingredient in medicinal teas for lost voice because of their high mucilage content, which helps to relieve irritation and inflammation.
Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant contains mucilage, which can help to soothe and protect irritated throat tissues.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: High mallow tea helps with lost voice because it contains mucilage, a substance that forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes in the throat. This film helps to reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief to a sore or scratchy throat. Additionally, high mallow tea has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help to loosen phlegm and expel it from the respiratory tract. Drinking high mallow tea can also help to hydrate the throat and vocal cords, which can become dry and irritated due to overuse or illness. By soothing, reducing inflammation, and hydrating the throat, high mallow tea can help to alleviate a lost voice and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: High mallow tea is slippery and soothes irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and providing relief to a sore or lost voice. This is due to its high mucilage content.
Anti-inflammatory: High mallow tea reduces inflammation in the throat, easing discomfort and promoting voice recovery. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Expectorant: High mallow tea helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and facilitating voice production. This is attributed to its saponin content.
Antitussive: High mallow tea can help suppress coughing, which can further irritate the throat and worsen a lost voice. This is due to the presence of coumarins.

Soothing: High mallow tea has a calming effect on the throat, reducing irritation and promoting healing. This is due to the tea's overall mix of soothing constituents, including mucilage, flavonoids, and coumarins.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried high mallow herb with 1 cup of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb particles.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of the high mallow tea daily, until the voice returns to normal.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High mallow leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for lost voice due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce irritation and swelling in the throat.
Flowers: The flowers of the high mallow plant contain mucilage, which helps to coat and soothe the throat, making them useful in teas for a lost voice.
Seeds: High mallow seeds are rich in mucilage and are often used in teas to help lubricate and soothe irritated throats, promoting voice recovery.
Roots: Although less commonly used, the roots of the high mallow plant contain mucilage and have expectorant properties, making them helpful in medicinal teas for a lost voice and clearing phlegm.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the high mallow plant are a good source of mucilage and have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory conditions, making them suitable for medicinal teas to restore a lost voice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coltsfoot tea helps with a lost voice because it contains mucilage, a substance that coats and soothes irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and providing relief. The tea also has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to expel mucus and reduce congestion in the respiratory tract. Furthermore, coltsfoot contains flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to protect the throat from damage and promoting healing. Drinking coltsfoot tea can help to alleviate the symptoms of a lost voice, such as hoarseness, dryness, and soreness, and can help to speed up the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Coltsfoot tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and mucilage, which help reduce inflammation in the throat, providing relief for a lost voice.
Expectorant: The demulcent and expectorant properties of coltsfoot tea, due to its mucilage content, help soothe irritated mucous membranes and expel excess mucus, alleviating a lost voice.
Soothing: Coltsfoot tea has a soothing effect on the throat, thanks to its mucilaginous and emollient constituents, providing comfort and reducing irritation caused by a lost voice.
Antitussive: Coltsfoot tea contains antitussive compounds, such as zaluzanin C, that help alleviate coughing, thereby reducing strain on the vocal cords and aiding in voice recovery.
Analgesic: Coltsfoot tea includes analgesic compounds, like tannins, that help alleviate pain and discomfort in the throat, providing relief for a lost or strained voice.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried coltsfoot leaves, letting it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, pouring the liquid into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day.
Discard any remaining tea after 24 hours.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the coltsfoot plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for a lost voice due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the coltsfoot plant are used in teas for a lost voice because they contain mucilage, which helps soothe irritated throat tissues.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the coltsfoot plant are used in medicinal teas for a lost voice as they are rich in saponins, which help loosen phlegm and relieve congestion.
Roots: The roots of the coltsfoot plant are used in teas for a lost voice because they contain marrubiin, a compound known to have analgesic and expectorant properties.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat lost voice with herbal teas is by choosing teas that reduce inflammation and soothe irritation. Slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root are excellent choices. These herbs create a protective coating on the throat, helping to reduce pain and swelling. Drinking these teas can also help keep the throat moist, which is crucial when dealing with a lost voice. However, it's important to note that while herbal teas can provide relief and support throat health, they should not replace medical treatment if the issue persists.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to a lost voice that are treated with herbal teas include hoarseness, sore throat, and cough. Herbal teas like slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root can help soothe irritated throat tissues and reduce inflammation. Throat coat tea, a blend of these herbs, is also commonly used. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties and can help relax throat muscles, while ginger tea can help reduce inflammation and relieve pain. Peppermint tea can soothe a sore throat and act as a natural decongestant. However, it is important to consult a healthcare provider if symptoms persist or worsen.
